Title: Starch granule initiation in Arabidopsis thaliana chloroplasts

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Protein Targeting To Starch family; carbohydrate-binding domain-containing protein
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/chloroplast; specific points at thylakoid membranes via MFP1-associated localization
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal starch granule initiation; elimination causes phenotype similar to ss4 with strongly reduced granule number
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes large reduction in granule number and altered starch morphology
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PTST2 interacts with SS4 and other initiation factors. 2. It likely delivers suitable maltooligosaccharide substrates to SS4. 3. Its elimination causes a severe starch initiation defect similar to ss4.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core nonenzymatic component of initiation machinery in this review.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering starch granule number through substrate delivery/localization mechanisms.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MAR-binding filament-like coiled-coil protein with predicted N-terminal transmembrane domain and C-terminal DNA-binding domain
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Thylakoid membrane-associated; associated with chloroplast nucleoids
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; localization and interaction studies
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss reduces starch granule number per chloroplast; granules are larger than WT but remain lenticular
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced granule number with altered size
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MFP1 is required for normal starch granule number. 2. It likely positions PTST2 at specific thylakoid membrane sites. 3. Its phenotype is milder and more developmentally uniform than ss4.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as spatial organizer rather than catalytic enzyme or transcriptional regulator.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to adjust spatial initiation sites and granule number.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Myosin-resembling chloroplast protein; also called PROTEIN INVOLVED IN STARCH INITIATION (PII1/MRC)
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Specific points of thylakoid membranes in chloroplasts
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; protein interaction and localization analysis; epistasis with ss5
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss reduces starch granule number per chloroplast while granules remain lenticular; likely supports initiation complex function
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced granule number and enlarged granules
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PII1 interacts with SS4 and SS5 and localizes to initiation foci. 2. It is required for proper starch granule initiation but probably not by substrate delivery. 3. It may support SS4 folding or interaction with other factors.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Localization of SS4 and PII1 does not depend on each other despite interaction.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for modifying initiation machinery assembly and granule number.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elimination causes a minor reduction in starch granule number in leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PTST3 contributes to starch granule initiation in Arabidopsis. 2. Its effect is weaker than PTST2. 3. Its molecular function remains unclear and interaction with SS4 has not been shown.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not well conserved phylogenetically; absent in several plant groups including grasses.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary modifier of starch granule number, but uncertain utility due to unclear mechanism.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Combined loss with PHS1 reduces starch granule number, indicating a role in maltose/MOS flux linked to granule initiation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark phase required; phenotype suppressed under continuous light
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong context-dependent metabolic defects when combined with phs1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MEX1-mediated maltose transport contributes to the precursor network feeding granule initiation. 2. mex1 phs1 double mutants have reduced granule number. 3. The effect depends strongly on photoperiod.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport by MEX1 is bidirectional according to cited literature.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to alter starch initiation indirectly via maltose flux, but likely pleiotropic.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Combined loss with PHS1 causes strongest reduction in starch granule number, often one granule per chloroplast
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Requires dark phase for phenotype; continuous light restores granule number toward WT
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe metabolic and growth phenotypes in double mutant background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DPE2 is part of the maltose metabolism network affecting starch granule initiation. 2. dpe2 phs1 double mutants show a drastic reduction in granule number. 3. The effect supports a central role for MOS supply in initiation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although cytosolic, DPE2 strongly affects plastidial granule initiation through whole-cell carbohydrate flux.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not ideal standalone target due to strong pleiotropy, but informative for precursor management.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AMY3 partially suppresses ss4 and ss3 ss4 phenotypes, indicating AMY3 normally depletes MOS that could otherwise support granule initiation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Amylase inhibition may alter starch turnover and growth
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AMY3 negatively influences availability of MOS for starch initiation. 2. Removing AMY3 partially restores granule formation in ss4-related mutant backgrounds. 3. Amylolytic activity therefore contributes to granule number control.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts indirectly by consuming potential primer MOS rather than by synthesizing starch.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential negative target to enhance initiation under compromised synthase backgrounds, but with possible turnover defects.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic analysis of gwd single mutant and dpe2 phs1 gwd background
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Blocking GWD-mediated starch degradation increases starch granule number, indicating starch breakdown influences granule number regulation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alters starch turnover and granule morphology
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWD is a key initiator of starch degradation that indirectly controls granule number. 2. Loss of GWD increases granule number in WT and in dpe2 phs1 background. 3. This supports coupling of degradation with initiation regulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phosphorylation by GWD also occurs during starch synthesis according to cited work.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can modify granule number via turnover control, but likely causes broad starch excess phenotypes.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT5 family glycosyltransferase; GT-B-fold; C-terminal GT5 and GT1 domains; long N-terminal coiled-coil region
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Starch granule initiation in chloroplast transitory starch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ADP-glucose and maltooligosaccharides (maltose/short MOS as acceptors)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated α-1,4-glucan primer/maltooligosaccharides leading to nascent starch granule
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucosyl residues from ADP-glucose to α-1,4-glucan acceptors, elongating maltooligosaccharides/amylopectin-like glucans during starch granule initiation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly elongates the primer that gives rise to the starch granule
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbohydrate reserve in plants; key calorie source for human diet; industrial renewable material
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast plastoglobule-associated specific points of thylakoid membranes
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PTST2; PII1/MRC; fibrillin 1a; fibrillin 1b; PHS1; SS4 (homodimer)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping (ss4); GFP translational fusion localization; heterologous enzyme assay in E. coli; protein interaction analyses; double/triple mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal starch granule initiation; loss causes one or two enlarged rounded starch granules per chloroplast and near absence of granules in young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for starch granule initiation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf developmental stage; photoperiod/continuous light versus light-dark cycles
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss leads to severe alteration of granule number, enlarged spherical granules, strong ADP-glucose accumulation, disturbed starch turnover and growth-related phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS4 is the central enzymatic factor for Arabidopsis chloroplast starch granule initiation. 2. It provides elongating activity needed to generate the primer/nascent glucan for granule formation. 3. Loss of SS4 drastically reduces granule number and alters granule morphology, although residual initiation can be supported by other enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper. SS4 localization depends on its N-terminus; active form appears to be a homodimer; mechanism appears broadly conserved in plants including storage starch systems.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating SS4 offers a route to alter starch granule number and morphology in crops, but likely requires coordinated engineering with partner proteins to avoid severe metabolic defects.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Starch synthase with GT5 and GT1 domains; long N-terminus with three carbohydrate-binding domains
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated α-1,4-glucans contributing to starch granule formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates glucan acceptors using ADP-glucose and can bind/elongate maltooligosaccharides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Can replace SS4 partially to initiate/elongate glucan primers when SS4 is absent
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mainly surrounding starch granule surface, not specific thylakoid membrane dots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double and triple mutant analysis (especially ss3 ss4)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Partially compensates for SS4 absence; ss3 ss4 double mutants mostly lack starch granules, indicating SS3 supports residual initiation in ss4 background
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined loss with SS4 causes extreme loss of granules/starch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS3 provides alternative elongating activity for granule formation when SS4 is absent. 2. Its mechanism appears distinct from canonical SS4-dependent initiation. 3. Loss of both SS3 and SS4 nearly abolishes starch granules.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No reported interaction with PTST2 or PII1; likely initiates via a distinct pathway enabled by carbohydrate-binding domains.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a backup or compensatory target when modifying starch initiation, though weaker than SS4.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Noncanonical SS isoform phylogenetically related to SS4
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Lacks catalytic glycosyltransferase activity; functions nonenzymatically in granule initiation-associated process
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; in vivo dimerization/interaction analysis; epistasis analysis with pii1 and ss4
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss reduces starch granule number per chloroplast, with milder phenotype than ss4; granules remain discoid but with irregular margins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS5 influences starch granule initiation despite lacking catalytic SS activity. 2. It interacts with PII1 and acts in the initiation machinery. 3. Its effect is weaker than that of SS4 and appears partly PII1-dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although phylogenetically related to SS4, SS5 acts via a noncanonical noncatalytic mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential modifier of starch granule number/morphology without directly altering catalytic flux.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Maltooligosaccharide supply for starch granule initiation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Maltooligosaccharides of DP>4 and glucose-1-phosphate/glucan acceptors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reversible glucosyl transfer between glucose-1-phosphate and α-glucans, extending maltooligosaccharides that may feed starch initiation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Substrate-supplying side route linked to initiation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor maltooligosaccharide pool used by initiation machinery
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbohydrate storage and night/day metabolic buffering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic analysis of phs1 single and double mutants (dpe2 phs1; mex1 phs1); protein interaction evidence with SS4
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential contributor to proper granule number under conditions where MOS supply is limiting; likely provides substrates required by initiation machinery
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key conditional control point for MOS supply to initiation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark phase versus continuous light; photoregime strongly alters phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation interacts with starch degradation defects to strongly reduce granule number and alter metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHS1 is not sufficient alone to alter initiation strongly, but becomes crucial in combination with defects in MOS/maltose metabolism. 2. It likely supplies maltooligosaccharides used during starch granule initiation. 3. PHS1 physically interacts with SS4, linking precursor supply to initiation machinery.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its role in starch synthesis versus degradation remains debated; review concludes it is essential for synthesis of substrates used by initiation machinery.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modulating precursor supply and granule initiation, but effects are highly context dependent on photoperiod and starch turnover background.
